block_goal: Extend the canonical Antarctica player-facing content slice beyond i16 using runtime-owned DTOs and bounded player-web rendering without reintroducing direct games/* reads from player-web.
architect_instructions_for_orchestrator: Run PM plan review before any task materialization.
Use the declared role runtime bindings and do not invent launcher, model, or tier values.
Materialize temporary task packets under .tmp/agent-workflow/cubica-antarctica-player-content-extension-example/ and remove accepted task files after PM acceptance.
Preserve the runtime-first dependency order: runtime/contracts before player-web rendering.
global_rules: Use Context7 when current library or framework behavior matters.
Do not introduce a generic engine, DSL, selector system, or reusable abstraction without a proven repeat use case.
Do not let apps/player-web read games/* directly as runtime truth.
Update PROJECT_STRUCTURE.json and repo-manifest.json for structural changes.
Update delivery-specific migration details in Gameplay Slice Records instead of ADRs.
If draft/Antarctica/GameFull.html must be consulted, use targeted scripts and extraction commands instead of whole-file prose reading.
global_acceptance: runtime-api remains the sole owner of game content loading for the delivered slice.
player-web renders the extended slice from runtime-owned DTOs or keeps the existing safe fallback.
Required verification commands pass for runtime-api, player-web, and the canonical slice.
Code, tests, and project documentation do not contradict each other.
global_invariants: Executable game logic ends in games/antarctica/game.manifest.json.
services/runtime-api plus apps/player-web remain the canonical runnable slice.
Delivery-specific bounded mechanics are documented in docs/architecture/gameplay-slices/, not in ADRs.
Architect owns plan truth, Orchestrator routes mechanically, PM owns acceptance for the block.

risk_policy.R0: mechanical local change without behavior expansion
risk_policy.R1: bounded behavior or DTO extension inside the canonical slice
risk_policy.R2: cross-boundary contract or reusable architecture change

slices.title: Extend runtime-owned Antarctica content projection
slices.goal: Expose the next bounded Antarctica opening slice through contracts and runtime content projection without introducing a new platform-wide API shape.
forbidden_moves: Do not create a new generic player-content DSL or generic engine layer.
Do not move content ownership out of services/runtime-api.
Do not describe a stable architecture change in a gameplay slice record or delivery tracker.
control_points: The DTO extension remains bounded to the current Antarctica slice and does not become a platform-wide abstraction.
Every new acceptance criterion maps to a runtime test, verification command, or explicit manual note for PM.
mandatory_tests: Add or update runtime-api integration coverage for the newly projected steps.
Keep manifest validation coverage aligned with the delivered content shape.
acceptance_criteria: GET /games/antarctica/player-content exposes the next bounded content slice needed by player-web.
Runtime validation and tests prove the new DTO shape is accepted and stable for the delivered slice.
Handoff and next-steps documentation describe the newly delivered boundary accurately.

tasks.goal: Extend manifest contracts and runtime-owned content projection for the next bounded Antarctica slice beyond i16.
inputs: Current runtime handoff and next-step priorities
implementation_notes: Keep the shape capability-first and bounded to the delivered Antarctica slice.
Prefer extending current DTO and validation paths over introducing new transport layers.
If legacy HTML must be consulted, use npm run antarctica:extract-step or other targeted extraction commands.
tests_to_write: Runtime integration tests for the next player-facing content DTO slice
Manifest validation tests for any newly required bounded shape
deliverables: Gameplay slice record for the delivered boundary
escalate_if: A reusable platform abstraction seems required
The DTO must break existing public contracts
The write scope must expand outside the canonical runtime slice

slices.title: Extend player-web rendering from runtime-owned content
slices.goal: Render the newly exposed Antarctica slice in apps/player-web using runtime-owned DTOs while preserving the existing fallback for unmigrated steps.
forbidden_moves: Do not make apps/player-web read games/* directly.
Do not couple UI state to draft/antarctica-nextjs-player structures.
Do not broaden the feature into a generic multi-game rendering engine.
control_points: The fallback path for not-yet-modeled steps remains safe.
mandatory_tests: Add or update player-web tests for the newly rendered current-step path.
acceptance_criteria: Player-web renders the newly delivered bounded Antarctica slice from the runtime DTO.
The existing fallback remains intact for steps outside the delivered slice.
Verification proves the canonical runtime plus web slice still works end to end.

tasks.goal: Extend the canonical player-web renderer to use the runtime-owned DTO for the next bounded Antarctica slice.
inputs: Gameplay slice record and next-step priorities
implementation_notes: Prefer local rendering extension over introducing cross-game UI abstractions.
Keep current-step state derived from runtime DTOs and session snapshot, not from direct repository file reads.
Retain a safe fallback for any step that is still outside the delivered slice.
tests_to_write: Player-web tests covering the newly rendered bounded path and fallback behavior
deliverables: Updated next steps if the visible slice boundary moved
escalate_if: The task requires direct games/* reads from player-web
The UI change needs a new reusable engine or selector system
The write scope must grow outside apps/player-web and the declared docs
